settings.text: Certified Public Accountants · Austin, TX
Accounting that reads like it was built for your business.
We handle the tax, bookkeeping, and advisory work that keeps small businesses and self-employed professionals moving. Thirty-minute discovery call, no commitment, straight answers.

settings.title: Four practice areas, one firm.
settings.description: Everything a small business or self-employed professional needs, handled by the same people who know your books.

settings.title: Tax preparation & filing
settings.description: Federal, state, and local returns for individuals and businesses. Quarterly estimated payments, multi-state compliance, and IRS correspondence handled end-to-end.

settings.title: Bookkeeping & payroll
settings.description: Monthly reconciliations, financial statements, and payroll run through QuickBooks or Xero. Your books are always current, always reviewed by a CPA.

settings.title: Advisory & forecasting
settings.description: Quarterly strategy meetings, cash-flow forecasting, and tax planning ahead of year-end. The work that helps you make decisions, not just report them.

settings.title: Audit & assurance support
settings.description: Audit preparation, reviewed statements, and representation when the IRS or a lender needs documentation. We speak their language so you don't have to.

settings.title: Different businesses, different books.
settings.description: We shape our engagements around how your business actually runs.

settings.title: Monthly close, done right
settings.text: Reconciled books by the 10th of each month, clean financials you can share with lenders or investors, and a CPA on the account — not a bookkeeper.

settings.title: Payroll & 1099s handled
settings.text: We run payroll, issue 1099s, and keep your multi-state obligations current. One firm, one point of contact.

settings.title: Sales tax across states
settings.text: Nexus tracking, registration, and filings in every state where you owe — so selling into a new market doesn't come with a compliance headache.

settings.title: Quarterly estimates
settings.text: Stop guessing what you owe the IRS. We calculate quarterly estimated payments and adjust as your income changes through the year.

settings.title: Schedule C, simplified
settings.text: We organize your receipts, track deductible mileage and home-office expenses, and file a return that holds up if the IRS asks.

settings.title: Retirement that keeps more
settings.text: SEP-IRA, Solo 401(k), and defined-benefit plans for the self-employed — picked, set up, and funded to cut your tax bill where it counts.

settings.title: Form 990 & compliance
settings.text: Annual 990 preparation, grant-fund tracking, and board-ready financials. We keep your exempt status clean and your trustees informed.

settings.title: Audit-ready at any time
settings.text: Monthly closes and restricted-fund tracking that keep you ready for an independent audit — not scrambling the week before.

settings.title: Grant & restricted funds
settings.text: Fund-level accounting that keeps restricted grant dollars separate, reportable, and defensible — the way funders expect to see them.
Our approach
A firm that answers the phone.

We're a small practice on purpose. Every engagement is led by a CPA, not passed to a junior. You'll know who does your books, who signs your return, and who picks up when you call.

We don't chase volume. We take on clients we can serve well — and we say no when we can't.

settings.title: From first call to ongoing partnership.
Discovery call
settings.description: A 30-minute conversation about your business, your books, and what you actually need help with. No prep required.

settings.title: Engagement letter
settings.description: We scope the work, agree on a flat monthly fee, and put it in writing. No hourly surprises, no scope creep.

settings.title: Onboarding
settings.description: We connect to your accounting software, organize what exists, and bring your books current. Usually takes two to three weeks.

settings.title: Ongoing partnership
settings.description: Monthly closes, quarterly check-ins, and year-round tax planning. You get a CPA on retainer, not a once-a-year appointment.

settings.text: A client's words
"They saved me from a mess of my own making, and I've handed them every return since."
Mara Jensen, founder — Cedar Creek Interiors

settings.title: Still have questions?
settings.text:
The Resources page has detailed answers on tax deadlines, deliverables, pricing, and what to bring to a first meeting.

Or skip straight to a call.

settings.question: Do you work with clients outside Texas?
settings.answer: Yes. Most of our clients are in Texas, but we work with small businesses and self-employed professionals in every state. Tax law is federal; state compliance is something we've handled in all 50 states.

settings.question: How does pricing work?
settings.answer: Flat monthly fees, agreed on in the engagement letter. Tax-only clients pay per return. We don't bill hourly for scoped work — a phone call should never come with a clock attached.

settings.question: Can you take over from my current accountant?
settings.answer: Yes. We handle the transition — requesting prior-year files, reconciling any gaps, and getting you current. It's a common first engagement.

settings.question: What do I need to bring to the first call?
settings.answer: Nothing. The discovery call is a conversation, not a review. If we decide to work together, we'll send a checklist of what to gather for onboarding.

settings.question: Do you handle IRS notices and audits?
settings.answer: Yes. All our CPAs and enrolled agents have full representation rights. If the IRS writes, we write back.

settings.question: What software do you use?
settings.answer: QuickBooks Online and Xero are our primary platforms. We'll migrate from spreadsheets, desktop QuickBooks, or another cloud system as part of onboarding if needed.

settings.text: Ready when you are.
Thirty minutes, no prep, no obligation.
